title: hedyotis capitulifera
intro: Hedyotis capitulifera, also known as the Chinese hedyotis or pu huang, is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its small white flowers and rounded leaves. Traditionally used in Chinese medicine, Hedyotis capitulifera is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating cough and sore throat, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Hedyotis capitulifera is often combined with other herbs and used in teas, infusions, or capsules to harness its purported medicinal and health benefits.
